evidence: The paper explicitly states "Permutation variable importance...were calculated for each model" (Methods). The main substantive claim—agricultural seasonality models achieve 0.99 vs 0.80 correlation with actual seasonal patterns—is grounded in model comparison and out-of-sample AUC/Brier scores, not variable importance ranking. Permutation importance is robust to impurity bias; the paper's use of spatial-block cross-validation and multiple corroboration methods further isolates conclusions from any single metric's limitations.
